dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4752 IS: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to increase criminal and civil penalties for unauthorized disclosure of taxpayer information, and for other purposes.
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Daines (for himself and Ms. Cortez Masto) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Finance

form.official-title: To amend the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 to increase criminal and civil penalties for unauthorized disclosure of taxpayer information, and for other purposes.

section.header: Modifications to penalties for unauthorized disclosures of taxpayer information

paragraph.text: Paragraphs (1), (2), (3), (4), and (5) of section 7213(a) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 are each amended by striking $5,000, or imprisonment of not more than 5 years each place it appears and inserting $250,000, or imprisonment of not more than 7 years.

paragraph.header: Failure of contractors to maintain safeguards

subparagraph.text: Part I of subchapter A of chapter 75 of subtitle F of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by inserting after section 7213A the following new section:

section.header: Failure of IRS contractors to maintain safeguards

subsection.text: In the case of a contractor of the Internal Revenue Service that willfully fails to implement or enforce any applicable requirement under section 6103 (or regulations prescribed thereunder) to protect the confidentiality of returns or return information, if such failure results in the unauthorized disclosure of returns or return information, such contractor shall be guilty of a felony and, upon conviction thereof, shall be fined the greater of—

paragraph.text: an amount equal to 25 percent of the total amount obligated under all contracts with the Internal Revenue Service during the first fiscal year in which such contractor was provided access to any returns or return information related to the unauthorized disclosure.

subsection.text: For purposes of this section, the term contractor of the Internal Revenue Service means any person described in section 6103(n) (including any officer or employee of such person) in connection with a written contract with the Internal Revenue Service.

subparagraph.text: The table of sections for part I of subchapter A of chapter 75 of subtitle F of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by inserting after the item relating to section 7213A the following new item:

toc.toc-entry: Sec. 7213B. Failure of IRS contractors to maintain safeguards.

clause.text: Section 6103(e)(11) of such Code is amended by inserting 7213B, after 7213A,.
Section 6105(d) of such Code is amended by inserting 7213B, after 7213A,.
Section 7431(e) of such Code is amended by striking the first sentence and inserting the following:

quoted-block.text: If any person is criminal charged by indictment or information with—

paragraph.text: inspection or disclosure of a taxpayer's return or return information in violation of—

subparagraph.text: subparagraph (B) of section 1030(a)(2) of title 18, United States Code, or

paragraph.text: failure to protect the confidentiality of returns or return information which results in the unauthorized disclosure of the taxpayer's return or return information in violation of section 7213B(a),

quoted-block.quoted-block-continuation-text: the Secretary shall notify such taxpayer as soon as practicable of such inspection or disclosure.

clause.text: Section 7513(c) of such Code is amended by striking section 7213 and inserting sections 7213 and 7213B.

subsection.text: Section 7431(c)(1)(A) of the Internal Revenue Code of 1986 is amended by striking $1,000 and inserting $5,000.
The amendments made by this section shall apply to disclosures or inspections made after the date of the enactment of this Act.
